Contact.!scriptable.description: In order to add the contact to your address book, you must queue it for insertion using Contact.add(). When you're done making changes to the address book you should call Contact.persistChanges() to persist the changes.

all.!scriptable.description: Fetches the contacts in the specified containers. A contact can be in only one container.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: containers
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Containers to fetch contacts from.

inGroups.!scriptable.description: Fetches the contacts in the specified contacts groups. A contact may belong to many groups.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: groups
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Groups to fetch contacts from.

add.!doc: Queues a contact to be added.
add.!scriptable.description: After you have created a contact, you must queue the contact to be added to the address book and invoke Contact.persistChanges() to persist the changes to the address book. For performance reasons, it is best to batch changes to the address book. Therefore you should queue all updates, insertions and removals of contacts and contacts groups to as large batches as possible and then call Contact.persistChanges() when you want to persist the changes to the address book.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: contact
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Contact to queue to be added.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: containerIdentifier
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Optional. Identifier of container to add the contact to. If null is specified, the contact will be added to the default container.

update.!doc: Queues an update to a contact.
update.!scriptable.description: After you have updated one or more properties on a contact, you must queue the contact to be updated and invoke Contact.persistChanges() to persist the changes to the address book. For performance reasons, it is best to batch changes to the address book. Therefore you should queue all updates, insertions and removals of contacts and contacts groups to as large batches as possible and then call Contact.persistChanges() when you want to persist the changes to the address book.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: contact
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Contact to queue to be updated.

delete.!doc: Queues a contact to be deleted.
delete.!scriptable.description: To delete a contact, you must queue the contact for deletion and invoke Contact.persistChanges() to persist the changes to the address book. For performance reasons, it is best to batch changes to the address book. Therefore you should queue all updates, insertions and removals of contacts and contacts groups to as large batches as possible and then call Contact.persistChanges() when you want to persist the changes to the address book.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: contact
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Contact to queue to be deleted.

persistChanges.!doc: Persist queued changes to the address book.
persistChanges.!scriptable.description: Call this function to persist changes queued with Contact.add(), Contact.update() and Contact.delete(). For performance reasons, it is best to batch changes to the address book. Therefore you should queue all updates, insertions and removals of contacts and contacts groups to as large batches as possible and then call Contact.persistChanges() when you want to persist the changes to the address book.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: identifier
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Identifier of the contacts container to fetch.

ContactsGroup.!scriptable.description: In order to add the group to your address book, you must queue it for insertion using ContactsGroup.add(). When you're done making changes to the address book you should call Contact.persistChanges() to persist the changes.

all.!scriptable.description: Fetches the contacts groups in the specified containers. A group can be in only one container.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: containers
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Container to fetch contacts groups from.

add.!doc: Queues a contacts group to be added.
add.!scriptable.description: After you have created a group, you must queue the group to be added to the address book and invoke Contact.persistChanges() to persist the changes to the address book. For performance reasons, it is best to batch changes to the address book. Therefore you should queue all updates, insertions and removals of contacts and contacts groups to as large batches as possible and then call Contact.persistChanges() when you want to persist the changes to the address book.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: group
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Contacts group to queue to be added.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: containerIdentifier
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Optional. Identifier of container to add the contacts group to. If null is specified, the group will be added to the default container.

update.!doc: Queues an update to a contacts group.
update.!scriptable.description: After you have updated one or more properties on a contacts group, you must queue the group to be updated and invoke Contact.persistChanges() to persist the changes to the address book. For performance reasons, it is best to batch changes to the address book. Therefore you should queue all updates, insertions and removals of contacts and contacts groups to as large batches as possible and then call Contact.persistChanges() when you want to persist the changes to the address book.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: group
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Contacts group to queue to be updated.

delete.!doc: Queues a contacts group to be deleted.
delete.!scriptable.description: To delete a contacts group, you must queue the group for deletion and invoke Contact.persistChanges() to persist the changes to the address book. For performance reasons, it is best to batch changes to the address book. Therefore you should queue all updates, insertions and removals of contacts and contacts groups to as large batches as possible and then call Contact.persistChanges() when you want to persist the changes to the address book.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: group
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Contacts group to queue to be deleted.

screenSize.!scriptable.description: The value is measured in points. For an explanation of the relationship between points and pixels, see the documentation of the screenScale() method. The value takes the device rotation into account, so the value will vary between portrait and landscape.

setAccuracyToHundredMeters.!doc: Sets accuracy to within hundred meters.

setAccuracyToKilometer.!doc: Sets accuracy to within one kilometer.

reverseGeocode.!scriptable.description: A reverse-geocoding request fetches information about the current location. The data is delivered by Apple's geocoding service.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Latitude of coordinate to fetch information about.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: longitude
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Longitude of coordinate to fetch information about.

removeAllPending.!scriptable.description:
Removes all notifications that have been scheduled from Scriptable and are waiting to be delivered.

Use with caution. This removes all notifications scheduled across all of your scripts and the action cannot be undone.

removeAllDelivered.!scriptable.description: Removes all notifications that have been scheduled from Scriptable and that are still displayed in the Notification Center of iOS.

removePending.!scriptable.description: Removes notifications with the specified identifiers. The notifications are only removed if they are pending, that is they have been scheduled and are waiting to be delivered. To remove delivered notifications, see Notification.removeDelivered().

importModule.!scriptable.description: Modules are imported by specifying the name of the file. For example, to import the file foo.js, call importModule('foo'). Including the file extension is optional. Scriptable will look for modules in the following directories, in order: 1. Relative to the file the module is imported into. 2. In Scriptables folder in iCloud if you have iCloud Drive enabled. This folder is accessible from the Files app. 3. In Scriptables "app group" folder which is not accessible to the user but your scripts are stored in this folder if you do not have iCloud Drive enabled. 4. In Scriptables local folder. This folder is accessible from the Files app. You can specify a file path rather than the name of a file e.g. importModule('/lib/foo'). If the path points to a directory, Scriptable will look for a file named index.js in the directory. The importModule function returns module.exports of the imported module. Consider the following file. let circle = importModule('circle') let r = 2 let area = circle.area(r) log('Area of circle: ' + area) The file imports the module circle.js which has the following contents. module.exports.area = (r) => { return Math.PI * Math.pow(r, 2) } module.exports.circumference = (r) => { return 2 * Math.PI * r } The circle.js module exports the functions area and circumference. For more information about modules, refer to the documentation on the module variable.

emailAddresses.!scriptable.description: An array of objects on the following form: { "identifier": "UUID-ABC-123", "label": "Home", "localizedLabel": "Home", "value": "my@example.com" } The identifier uniquely identifies the email address on this device. The label is a description of the email address and the value holds the email address itself. When updating this property, you must set the entire array of email addresses that you would like to store on the contact. Each value in the array must have the "value" key. The other keys are optional.

The label is a description of the phone number and the value holds the phone number itself. When updating this property, you must set the entire array of phone numbers that you would like to store on the contact. Each value in the array must have the "value" key. The other keys are optional.

The label is a description of the phone number and the value holds the phone number itself. When updating this property, you must set the entire array of postal addresses that you would like to store on the contact. The "identifier" key is optional.

The label is a description of the social profile, the service is the social profile's service name, the URL contains a link to the social profile, the userIdentifier is the identifier of the social profile and the username is the name for the social profile. When updating this property, you must set the entire array of social profiles that you would like to store on the contact. The "identifier" key is optional.

isPostalAddressesAvailable.!doc: Whether or not postal addresses are available.
isPostalAddressesAvailable.!scriptable.description: The postalAddresses property may not be available if the container does not support it. In that case any value set on the property will be ignored.

isSocialProfilesAvailable.!doc: Whether or not social profiles are available.
isSocialProfilesAvailable.!scriptable.description: The socialProfiles property may not be available if the container does not support it. In that case any value set on the property will be ignored.

isImageAvailable.!doc: Whether or not image is available.
isImageAvailable.!scriptable.description: The image property may not be available if the container does not support it. In that case any value set on the property will be ignored.

isNoteAvailable.!doc: Whether or not note is available.
isNoteAvailable.!scriptable.description: The note property may not be available if the container does not support it. In that case any value set on the property will be ignored.

isURLAddressesAvailable.!doc: Whether or not URL addresses are available.
isURLAddressesAvailable.!scriptable.description: The urlAddresses property may not be available if the container does not support it. In that case any value set on the property will be ignored.

isOrganizationNameAvailable.!doc: Whether or not organization name is available.
isOrganizationNameAvailable.!scriptable.description: The organizationName property may not be available if the container does not support it. In that case any value set on the property will be ignored.

isDepartmentNameAvailable.!doc: Whether or not department name is available.
isDepartmentNameAvailable.!scriptable.description: The departmentName property may not be available if the container does not support it. In that case any value set on the property will be ignored.

isJobTitleAvailable.!doc: Whether or not job title is available.
isJobTitleAvailable.!scriptable.description: The jobTitle property may not be available if the container does not support it. In that case any value set on the property will be ignored.

isDatesAvailable.!doc: Whether or not dates are available.
isDatesAvailable.!scriptable.description: The date property may not be available if the container does not support it. In that case any value set on the property will be ignored.

addMember.!doc: Adds a contact to the group.
addMember.!scriptable.description: In order to persist the change, you should call Contact.persistChanges(). It is important that the contact is added to the address book. To add the contact to the address book, you should queue it for insertion using Contact.add() before persisting the changes.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: contact
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Contact to add to the group.

removeMember.!doc: Removes a contact from the group.
removeMember.!scriptable.description: In order to persist the change, you should call Contact.persistChanges(). It is important that the contact is added to the address book. To add the contact to the address book, you should queue it for insertion using Contact.add() before persisting the changes.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: contact
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Contact to add to the group.

toRawString.!doc: Creates a string from the data.
toRawString.!scriptable.description: The data is assumed to represent a UTF8 encoded string. If the string is not UTF8 encoded string, the function will return null.

toBase64String.!scriptable.description: Creates a base64 encoded string from the data.

string.!doc: Creates a string from a date.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: date
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: The date to convert to a string.

date.!doc: Creates a date from a string.
date.!scriptable.description: Uses the date formatters configuration to parse the string into a date. If the string cannot be parsed with the date foramtters configuration, the function will return null.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: str
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: The string to parse into a date.

useNoDateStyle.!doc: Use no style for the date.
useNoDateStyle.!scriptable.description: This will remove the date from the formatted string.

useShortDateStyle.!doc: Use a short style for the date.
useShortDateStyle.!scriptable.description: Dates with a short style are typically numeric only e.g. "08/23/19".

useMediumDateStyle.!doc: Use a medium style for the date.
useMediumDateStyle.!scriptable.description: Dates with a medium style usually includes abbreviations, e.g. "Aug 23, 2019" or "7:16:42 PM".

useLongDateStyle.!doc: Use a long style for the date.
useLongDateStyle.!scriptable.description: Dates with a long style usually includes a full text, e.g. "August 23, 2019".

useFullDateStyle.!doc: Use a full style for the date.
useFullDateStyle.!scriptable.description: Dates with a full style includes all details, e.g. "Friday, August 23, 2019 AD".

useNoTimeStyle.!doc: Use no style for the time.
useNoTimeStyle.!scriptable.description: This will remove the time from the formatted string.

useShortTimeStyle.!doc: Use a short style for the time.
useShortTimeStyle.!scriptable.description: Times with a short style are typically numeric only but also includes the period for 12-hour clocks, e.g. "7:17 PM".

useMediumTimeStyle.!doc: Use a short style for the time.
useMediumTimeStyle.!scriptable.description: Times with a medium style usually includes abbreviations, e.g. "7:16:42 PM".

useLongTimeStyle.!doc: Use a long style for the time.
useLongTimeStyle.!scriptable.description: Times with a long style usually includes a full text, e.g. "7:16:42 PM PST".

useFullTimeStyle.!doc: Use a full style for the time.
useFullTimeStyle.!scriptable.description: Times with a full style includes all details, e.g. "7:16:42 PM Pacific Standard Time".

dateFormat.!doc: Date format to be used by the formatter.
dateFormat.!scriptable.description: Sets a fixed format to be used by the formatter. For example the date "2019-08-26 16:47" can be represented using the format "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm". When converting dates to strings, it's advised to use some of the predefined formats for dates and times that can be applied using functions on the formatter, e.g. useMediumDateStyle() and useMediumTimeStyle(). Year: - y: Year with no padding. Example: "2019" - yy: Year with two zeros. Adds padding if necessary. Example: "19" - yyyy: Year with a minimum of four digits. Adds padding if necessary. Example: "2019" Quarter: - Q: Quarter of the year. Example: "4" - QQQQ: Quarter spelled out. Example: "4th quarter" Month: - M: Numeric month of the year. Example: "1" - MM: Numeric month of the year. Adds padding if necessary. Example: "01" - MMM: Shorthand name of the month. Example: "Jan" - MMMM: Full name of the month. Example: "January" - MMMMM: Narrow name of the month. Example: "J" Day: - d: Day of the month. Example: "9" - dd: Day of the month. Adds padding if necessary. Example: "09" - F: Day of the week. Example: "3rd Friday in August" - E: Day of the week. Example: "Fri" - EEEE: Full name of the day. Example: "Friday" - EEEEE: Narrow day of the week. Example: "F" Hour: - h: Hour on a 12-hour clock. Example: "9" - hh: Hour on a 12-hour clock. Adds padding if necessary. Example: "09" - H: Hour on a 24-hour clock. Example: "21" - HH: Hour on a 24-hour clock. Adds padding if necessary. Example: "21" - a: AM/PM for times on a 12-hour clock. Example: "PM" Minute: - m: Minute. Example: "7" - mm: Minute. Adds padding if necessary. Example: "07" Second: - s: Seconds. Example: "4" - ss: Seconds. Adds padding if necessary. Example: "04" - SSS: Milliseconds. Example: "384" Time zone: - zzz: Three letter name of the time zone. Falls back to GMT-08:00 if the name is unknown. Example: "CST" - zzzz: Full name of the time zone. Falls back to GMT-08:00 if the name is unknown. Example: "Central Standard Time" - Z: Time zone in RFC 822 GMT format. Also matches a literal Z for Zulu (UTC) time. Example: "-0600" - ZZZZ: Time zone with abbreviation and offset. Example: "CST-06:00" - ZZZZZ: Time zone in ISO 8601 format. Example: "-06:00" A great resource for experimenting with date formats is nsdateformatter.com developed by Ben Scheirman.

locale.!scriptable.description: The locale should be specified using a string identifier, e.g. "en", "it" or "da". When no locale is set, the formatter will use the current locale of the device.

pickTime.!doc: Presents the date picker displaying hours and minutes.
pickTime.!scriptable.description: Use the method to pick a time. The date picker will display hours and minutes and, depending on the locale of the device, an AM/PM designation. The returned date will be the current date with the hour and minute set to the selected values. Use the initialDate property to set the initially selected date.

pickDate.!doc: Presents the date picker displaying day, month and year.
pickDate.!scriptable.description: Use the method to pick a date. The date picker will display the, day, month and year. Use the initialDate property to set the initially selected date.

pickDateAndTime.!doc: Presents the date picker displaying date and time.
pickDateAndTime.!scriptable.description: Use the method to pick a date and a time. The date picker will display the day, month, year, hour, minutes and, depending on the locale of the device, an AM/PM designation. Use the initialDate property to set the initially selected date.

pickCountdownDuration.!doc: Presents the date picker for selecting the duration of a countdown.
pickCountdownDuration.!scriptable.description: Use the method to pick the duration of a countdown, e.g. a timer. The date picker will display hours and minutes. Use the countdownDuration property to set the initially selected duration.

minimumDate.!doc: Minimum date that is selected in the picker.
minimumDate.!scriptable.description: The minimum date, along with the maximum date, specifies the valid date range. The minimum and maximum dates are ignored if the minimum date is greater than the maximum date. The dates are also ignored in countdown-timer mode.

maximumDate.!doc: Maximum date that is selected in the picker.
maximumDate.!scriptable.description: The maximum date, along with the minimum date, specifies the valid date range. The minimum and maximum dates are ignored if the minimum date is greater than the maximum date. The dates are also ignored in countdown-timer mode.

countdownDuration.!doc: Countdown duration displayed by the date picker.
countdownDuration.!scriptable.description: Use this property to get and set the duration of a countdown when calling the pickCountDownDuration() function to present the picker. The default value is zero and the maximum value is 23:59 (86,399 seconds).

minuteInterval.!doc: Interval at which the date picker displays minutes.
minuteInterval.!scriptable.description: Use the property to set the interval of the minute wheel. The default and minimum value is 1 and the maximum value is 30.

initialDate.!scriptable.description: Use this property to specify the initially selected date and time when picking a date, a time or both using date picker. If no date is specified, the current date and time will be selected initially. Be aware that this property does not hold the selected date after the date picker has been dismissed. The promises returned by pickTime(), pickDate() and PickDateAndTime() carries the selected date.

getImage.!scriptable.description: Call this to retrieve the image you have drawn to the context.

drawImageInRect.!doc: Draws an image in the specified rect.
drawImageInRect.!scriptable.description: Draws the image in the rectangle. The image will be scaled to fit within the rectangle.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: image
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Image to draw.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: rect
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Rectangle to draw the image in.

drawImageAtPoint.!doc: Draws an image at the specified point.
drawImageAtPoint.!scriptable.description: Draws the image at the point. The top-left corner of the image will be drawn at the specified point.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: image
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Image to draw.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: point
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Point at which to draw top-left corner of the image.

setFillColor.!scriptable.description: Sets the fill color to be used when performing a fill operation. Any fill operation performed afterwards will fill with the specified color until another call to setFillColor is made.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: color
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Color to set for filling.

setStrokeColor.!scriptable.description: Sets the stroke color to be used when performing a stroke operation. Any stroke operation performed afterwards will stroke with the specified color until another call to setStrokeColor is made.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: color
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Color to set for stroking.

setLineWidth.!doc: Sets the line width for stroking.
setLineWidth.!scriptable.description: Sets the line width to be used when performing a stroke operation.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: width
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Line width to use for stroking.

fill.!scriptable.description: Fills the rectangle with the color set when calling setFillColor.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: rect
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Rectangle to fill.

fillRect.!scriptable.description: Fills the rectangle with the color set when calling setFillColor.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: rect
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Rectangle to fill.

fillEllipse.!scriptable.description: Fills the ellipse that fits within the supplied rectangle with the color set when calling setFillColor.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: rect
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Rectangle incapsulating the ellipse to fill.

stroke.!scriptable.description: Draws a line around the rectangle using the color set when calling setStrokeColor. The line will have the width set when calling setLineWidth.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: rect
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Rectangle to stroke.

strokeRect.!scriptable.description: Draws a line around the rectangle using the color set when calling setStrokeColor. The line will have the width set when calling setLineWidth.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: rect
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Rectangle to stroke.

strokeEllipse.!scriptable.description: Draws a line around the ellipse that fits within the supplied rectangle. The line will have the color set when calling setStrokeColor and the width set when calling setLineWidth.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: rect
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Rectangle incapsulating the ellipse to stroke.

addPath.!doc: Adds a path to the context.
addPath.!scriptable.description: After adding a path to the context, the path can be stroked or filled by calling strokePath and fillPath. Note that only the path that was added latest will be affected by calls to strokePath and fillPath.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: path
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path to add to the context.

strokePath.!doc: Strokes the path that was added the latest.
strokePath.!scriptable.description: The path that was added the latest to the context is stroked with the color set using setStrokeColor and the line width set using setLineWidth.

fillPath.!doc: Fills the path that was added the latest.
fillPath.!scriptable.description: The path that was latest added to the context is filled with the color set using setFillColor.

drawText.!scriptable.description: Call this to draw a text string to the context. The top-left of the text will be drawn at the specified position.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: text
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Text to draw.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: pos
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Position to draw the top-left of the text.

drawTextInRect.!scriptable.description: Call this to draw a text string in a rectangle. Specify how the text should be aligned within the rectangle by calling setTextAlignedLeft, setTextAlignedCenter or setTextAlignedRight before drawing the text.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: text
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Text to draw.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: rect
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Rectangle to draw text in.

setFont.!doc: Sets the font to use when drawing text.
setFont.!scriptable.description: Sets the font to be used when drawing texts to the context.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: font
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Font to use when drawing text.

setTextColor.!doc: Sets the text color used when drawing text.
setTextColor.!scriptable.description: Sets the text color to be used when drawing text strings to the context.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: color
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Color to use when drawing text.

setTextAlignedLeft.!doc: Specifies that texts should be left aligned.
setTextAlignedLeft.!scriptable.description: Sets text alignment to left. Texts drawn after calling this will be left aligned inside the provided rectangle.

setTextAlignedCenter.!doc: Specifies that texts should be center aligned.
setTextAlignedCenter.!scriptable.description: Sets text alignment to center. Texts drawn after calling this will be center aligned inside the provided rectangle.

setTextAlignedRight.!doc: Specifies that texts should be right aligned.
setTextAlignedRight.!scriptable.description: Sets text alignment to right. Texts drawn after calling this will be right aligned inside the provided rectangle.

size.!scriptable.description:
Specifies the size of the canvas on which you are drawing. The image returned by getImage() will have this exact size,
except if respectScreenScale is true.

respectScreenScale.!doc: Enable to respect the scale of the screen.
respectScreenScale.!scriptable.description:
Devices have a screen scale that is used to convert between the logical coordinate space and the device coordinate space. For example, retina screens have a screen scale of 2 or 3 meaning that one point in the logical coordinate space is represented by four or nine pixels. Respecting the screen scale will multiply the specified size of the canvas by the screen scale. For example a canvas of size 200 by 200 will be 600 by 600 when the image is rendered on a retina screen with a screen scale of 3.
When respecting the screen scale is disabled, you may experience that your images looks blurry because essentially the size you have specified will be stretched when rendered on the screen. Default is false.

opaque.!doc: Determines whether the context is opaque.
opaque.!scriptable.description: When enabled your image will be rendered opaque. Default is true.

read.!doc: Read contents of a file as data.
read.!scriptable.description: Reads the contents of the file specified by the file path as raw data. To read the file as a string see readString(filePath) and to read it as an image see readImage(filePath). The function will error if the file does not exist or if it exists in iCloud but has not been download. Use fileExists(filePath) to check if a file exists and downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath) to download the file. Note that it is always safe to call downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath), even if the file is stored locally on the device.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of the file to read.

readString.!doc: Read contents of a file as string.
readString.!scriptable.description: The function will error if the file does not exist or if it exists in iCloud but has not been download. Use fileExists(filePath) to check if a file exists and downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath) to download the file. Note that it is always safe to call downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath), even if the file is stored locally on the device.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of the file to read.

readImage.!doc: Read contents of a file as an image.
readImage.!scriptable.description: Reads the contents of the file specified by the file path and converts it to an image. The function will error if the file does not exist or if it exists in iCloud but has not been download. Use fileExists(filePath) to check if a file exists and downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath) to download the file. Note that it is always safe to call downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath), even if the file is stored locally on the device.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of the file to read.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to write to.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: content
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Data to write to disk.

writeString.!doc: Write a string to a file.
writeString.!scriptable.description: Writes the content to the specified file path on disk. If the file does not already exist, it will be created. If the file already exists the contents of the file will be overwritten with the new content.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to write to.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: content
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Content to write to disk.

writeImage.!doc: Write an image to a file.
writeImage.!scriptable.description: Writes the image to the specified file path on disk. If the file does not already exist, it will be created. If the file already exists the contents of the file will be overwritten with the new content.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to write to.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: image
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Image to write to disk.

remove.!scriptable.description: Removes the file at the specified path. Use with caution. Removed files cannot be restored.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to remove.

move.!scriptable.description:
Moves the file from the source path to the destination path.
Caution: This operation will replace any existing file at the the destination.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: sourceFilePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of the file to move.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: destinationFilePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path to move the file to.

copy.!scriptable.description:
Copies the file from the source path to the destination path.
If a file already exists at the destination file path, the operation will fail and the file will not be copied.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: sourceFilePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of the file to copy.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: destinationFilePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path to copy the file to.

fileExists.!scriptable.description: Checks if the file exists at the specified file path. Checking this before moving or copying to a destination can be a good idea as those operations will replace any existing file at the destination file path.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: File path to examine.

isDirectory.!doc: Checks if a path points to a directory.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: path
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path to examine.

createDirectory.!doc: Creates a directory at the specified path.
createDirectory.!scriptable.description: You can optionally create all intermediate directories.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: path
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of directory to create.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: intermediateDirectories
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Whether to create all intermediate directories. Defaults to false.

temporaryDirectory.!scriptable.description:
Used to retrieve the path of a temporary directory on disk. 
Data persisted in a temporary directory will generally live shorter than data persisted in the cache directory.

The operating system may at any time delete files stored in this directory
and therefore you should not rely on it for long time storage.
If you need long time storage, see documentsDirectory() or libraryDirectory(). This directory is not shared between the app, the action extension and Siri.

cacheDirectory.!scriptable.description:
Used to retrieve the path of a cache directory on disk. 
The operating system may at any time delete files stored in this directory
and therefore you should not rely on it for long time storage.

Data persisted in the cache directory will generally live longer than data persisted in a temporary directory.

If you need long time storage, see documentsDirectory() or libraryDirectory(). This directory is not shared between the app, the action extension and Siri.

documentsDirectory.!scriptable.description: Used to retrieve the path to the documents directory. Your scripts are stored in this directory. If you have iCloud enabled, your scripts will be stored in the documents directory in iCloud otherwise they will be stored in the local documents directory. The directory can be used for long time storage. Documents stored in this directory can be accessed using the Files app. Files stored in the local documents directory will not appear in the Files app.

libraryDirectory.!scriptable.description: Used to retrieve the path to the library directory. The directory can be used for long time storage. Documents stored in this directory cannot be accessed using the Files app.

joinPath.!scriptable.description: Joins two paths to created one path. For example to join the path to a directory with the name of a file. This is the suggested approach for creating new file paths passed to the read and write functions of a FileManager.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: lhsPath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Left-hand side part of the new path.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: rhsPath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Right-hand side part of the new path.

allTags.!doc: Reads all tags from a file.
allTags.!scriptable.description: The tags are read from the file at the specified path. Tags can either be read, added and removed using the Files app by using the APIs provided by a FileManager. The function will error if the file does not exist or if it exists in iCloud but has not been download. Use fileExists(filePath) to check if a file exists and downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath) to download the file. Note that it is always safe to call downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath), even if the file is stored locally on the device.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to read tags from.

addTag.!doc: Adds a tag to a file.
addTag.!scriptable.description: A tag can only be added to a file once. It is not possible to specify a color for the tag. You can create the tags using the Files app to specify the color and then add them to files afterwards using the FileManager API. The function will error if the file does not exist or if it exists in iCloud but has not been download. Use fileExists(filePath) to check if a file exists and downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath) to download the file. Note that it is always safe to call downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath), even if the file is stored locally on the device.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to add the tag to.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: tag
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Tag to add. This can be an existing tag or a new tag.

removeTag.!doc: Removes a tag from a file.
removeTag.!scriptable.description: The function will error if the file does not exist or if it exists in iCloud but has not been download. Use fileExists(filePath) to check if a file exists and downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath) to download the file. Note that it is always safe to call downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath), even if the file is stored locally on the device.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to remove the tag from.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: tag
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Tag to remove.

readExtendedAttribute.!doc: Reads an extended attribute from a file.
readExtendedAttribute.!scriptable.description: Extended attributes are metadata that can be stored on a file. Note that extended attributes are not synced with iCloud. The function will return null if the attribute does not exist. The function will error if the file does not exist or if it exists in iCloud but has not been download. Use fileExists(filePath) to check if a file exists and downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath) to download the file. Note that it is always safe to call downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath), even if the file is stored locally on the device.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to read extended attribute from.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: name
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Name of the extended attribute to read.

writeExtendedAttribute.!type: fn(filePath: string, value: string, name: string)
writeExtendedAttribute.!doc: Writes an extended attribute to a file.
writeExtendedAttribute.!scriptable.description: Extended attributes are metadata that can be stored on a file. Note that extended attributes are not synced with iCloud. The function will error if the file does not exist or if it exists in iCloud but has not been download. Use fileExists(filePath) to check if a file exists and downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath) to download the file. Note that it is always safe to call downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath), even if the file is stored locally on the device.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to add an extended attribute to.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: value
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Value of the extended attribute.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: name
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Name of the extended attribute. This is used to retrieve the value at a later point.

removeExtendedAttribute.!doc: Removes an extended attribute from a file.
removeExtendedAttribute.!scriptable.description: Extended attributes are metadata that can be stored on a file. Note that extended attributes are not synced with iCloud. The function will error if the file does not exist or if it exists in iCloud but has not been download. Use fileExists(filePath) to check if a file exists and downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath) to download the file. Note that it is always safe to call downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath), even if the file is stored locally on the device.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to add an extended attribute to.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: name
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Name of the extended attribute to remove.

allExtendedAttributes.!doc: Reads all extended attributes on a file.
allExtendedAttributes.!scriptable.description: Extended attributes are metadata that can be stored on a file. Note that extended attributes are not synced with iCloud.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to read extended attributes from.

getUTI.!doc: Gets the UTI of the specified file.
getUTI.!scriptable.description: The Uniform Type Identifier is a string that identifies the type of file.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to get UTI of.

listContents.!scriptable.description: Lists all the contents in the specified directory. The returned array contains filenames to all files and directories in the specified directory.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: directoryPath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path to directory.

fileName.!scriptable.description:
Takes a file path and returns the name of the file. Also supports getting the name of a directory.
The returned file name optionally includes the extension of the file.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: path of file to get name of.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: includeFileExtension
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Whether or not the file extension should be included. Defaults to false.

fileExtension.!scriptable.description: Takes a file path and returns the extension of the file, e.g. ".jpg" or ".js". Returns en empty string for directories.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to get extension from.

bookmarkedPath.!doc: Get path to a bookmarked file or folder.
bookmarkedPath.!scriptable.description:
Gets the path to a bookmarked file or filder. Use file bookmarks to access files and folders outside Scriptables documents directory.

You can edit your file bookmarks from Scriptables settings.

The function will throw an error if the bookmark doesn't exist.

Please beware that bookmarks created from Scriptables settings only can be used when running a script in the app and not from the Share Sheet, Siri and Shortcuts. If you wish to use a bookmark from Siri or the Shortcuts app, the bookmark must be created using Scriptables "Create File Bookmark" shortcut action using the Shortcuts app.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: name
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Name of bookmark to create path for.

bookmarkExists.!scriptable.description:
Checks if a file bookmark exists with the specified name.

You can edit your file bookmarks from Scriptables settings.

Please beware that bookmarks created from Scriptables settings only can be used when running a script in the app and not from the Share Sheet, Siri and Shortcuts. If you wish to use a bookmark from Siri or the Shortcuts app, the bookmark must be created using Scriptables "Create File Bookmark" shortcut action using the Shortcuts app.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: name
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Name of bookmark.

downloadFileFromiCloud.!doc: Download file from iCloud if necessary.
downloadFileFromiCloud.!scriptable.description: Downloads the file from iCloud if it have not already been downloaded. If you pass in a path to a file that is not stored in iCloud, the returned promise will be resolved immediately making it safe to pass in any file path.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to download from iCloud.

isFileStoredIniCloud.!doc: Checks if a file is stored in iCloud.
isFileStoredIniCloud.!scriptable.description: Checks if a file is stored in iCloud or locally on the device. The function returns false if the file does not exist. Check if a file exists using fileExists(filePath)

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file.

isFileDownloaded.!doc: Checks if a file have been downloaded.
isFileDownloaded.!scriptable.description: If a file is stored in iCloud and it has not been downloaded, this function returns false. In that case, the file can be downloaded using downloadFileFromiCloud(filePath. If the file is not stored in iCloud but rather locally on the device, this function returns true. The function returns false if the file does not exist. Check if a file exists using fileExists(filePath)

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file.

creationDate.!doc: Reads the creation date of a file.
creationDate.!scriptable.description: The returned value will be null if the creation date cannot be read.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file.

modificationDate.!doc: Reads the modification date of a file.
modificationDate.!scriptable.description: The returned value will be null if the modification date cannot be read.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file.

fileSize.!doc: Size of the file in kilobytes.
fileSize.!scriptable.description: The returned value will be null if the file size cannot be read.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file.

allFileBookmarks.!doc: Reads all file bookmarks created in settings.
allFileBookmarks.!scriptable.description: File bookmarks are used to bookmark a file or a folder and read or write to it later. File bookmarks are created from Scriptables settings. This function returns all file bookmarks as an array of objects that take the following form. { "name": "My Bookmark", "source": "host" } The source can either be host for file bookmarks that can be used in the app or siri_shortcuts for file bookmarks that can be used in Siri and Shortcuts.

size.!doc: Size of the image in pixels.

colors.!scriptable.description: The array of colors should include the same amount of elements as the gradients locations property.

locations.!scriptable.description: Each location should be a value in the range of 0 to 1 and indicates the location of each color in the gradients colors array. The array of locations should include the same amount of elements as the gradients colors property.

startPoint.!scriptable.description: The normalized starting point of the gradient. The endPoint and startPoint together controls the direction of the gradient. The X and Y component should each range from 0 to 1. Defaults to (0, 1).

endPoint.!scriptable.description: The normalized ending point of the gradient. The endPoint and startPoint together controls the direction of the gradient. The X and Y component should each range from 0 to 1. Defaults to (0, 1).

addText.!scriptable.description: Adds a text element to the widget. Use the properties on the returned element to style the text.

addDate.!scriptable.description: Adds a date element to the widget. Use the properties on the returned element to style the date.

addImage.!scriptable.description: Adds an image element to the widget. Use the properties on the returned element to style the image.

addSpacer.!scriptable.description: Adds a spacer to the widget. This can be used to offset the content vertically in the widget.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: length
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Length of the spacer. Pass null to create a spacer with a flexible length.

addStack.!scriptable.description: Adds a stack to the widget. Stacks layout elements horizontally by default.

setPadding.!type: fn(top: number, leading: number, bottom: number, trailing: number)
setPadding.!scriptable.description: Sets the padding on each side of the widget.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: top
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Padding on the top edge.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: leading
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Padding on the leading edge.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: bottom
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Padding on the bottom edge.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: trailing
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Padding on the trailing edge.

useDefaultPadding.!scriptable.description: Configure the widget to use the default padding. Any padding previously defined with setPadding() will be discarded.

presentSmall.!doc: Presents a preview of the widget.
presentSmall.!scriptable.description:
The widget is presented in its small size.

Widgets on the Home Screen are updated periodically so while working on your widget you may want to preview it in the app.

presentMedium.!doc: Presents a preview of the widget.
presentMedium.!scriptable.description:
The widget is presented in its medium size.

Widgets on the Home Screen are updated periodically so while working on your widget you may want to preview it in the app.

presentLarge.!doc: Presents a preview of the widget.
presentLarge.!scriptable.description:
The widget is presented in its large size.

Widgets on the Home Screen are updated periodically so while working on your widget you may want to preview it in the app.

presentExtraLarge.!doc: Presents a preview of the widget.
presentExtraLarge.!scriptable.description:
The widget is presented in its extra large size.

Widgets on the Home Screen are updated periodically so while working on your widget you may want to preview it in the app.

The extra large widget is available on iPads running iOS 15 and newer.

presentAccessoryInline.!doc: Presents a preview of the widget.
presentAccessoryInline.!scriptable.description:
The widget is presented in its accessory inline size that suitable to be displayed above the clock on the Lock Screen.

The preview is an estimated representation of the widget and is not accurate. Widgets placed on the Lock Screen change appearance based on the wallpaper and tint color of the Lock Screen, and as such, they cannot be previewed accurately in Scriptable.

Widgets on the Lock Screen are updated periodically so while working on your widget you may want to preview it in the app.

The accessory inline widget is available on iPhones running iOS 16 and newer and can display a single image and a single text. Any additional elements will be filtered away during presentation.

presentAccessoryCircular.!doc: Presents a preview of the widget.
presentAccessoryCircular.!scriptable.description:
The widget is presented in its circular accessory size that suitable to be displayed above the clock on the Lock Screen.

The preview is an estimated representation of the widget and is not accurate. Widgets placed on the Lock Screen change appearance based on the wallpaper and tint color of the Lock Screen, and as such, they cannot be previewed accurately in Scriptable.

Widgets on the Lock Screen are updated periodically so while working on your widget you may want to preview it in the app.

The circular accessory widget is available on iPhones running iOS 16 and newer.

presentAccessoryRectangular.!doc: Presents a preview of the widget.
presentAccessoryRectangular.!scriptable.description:
The widget is presented in its rectangular accessory size that suitable to be displayed above the clock on the Lock Screen.

The preview is an estimated representation of the widget and is not accurate. Widgets placed on the Lock Screen change appearance based on the wallpaper and tint color of the Lock Screen, and as such, they cannot be previewed accurately in Scriptable.

Widgets on the Lock Screen are updated periodically so while working on your widget you may want to preview it in the app.

The rectangular accessory widget is available on iPhones running iOS 16 and newer.

backgroundColor.!scriptable.description: Defaults to a solid color in widgets placed on the Home Screen and a transparent color placed on the Lock Screen.

addAccessoryWidgetBackground.!doc: Whether to use an accessory widget background.
addAccessoryWidgetBackground.!scriptable.description:
Enable to add an adaptive background that provides a standard appearance based on the widget's environment. Defaults to false.

This is only available starting from iOS 16.

spacing.!scriptable.description: Specifies the spacing between elements in the widget. You can also use the addSpacer() function on the widget to add spacing between elements. Defaults to 0.

url.!scriptable.description: The URL will be opened when the widget is tapped. This will override any behavior defined in the configuration of the widget. E.g. if the widget is configured to run the script when interacting with the widget but a URL is set the URL will take precedence.

refreshAfterDate.!doc: Earliest date to refresh the widget.
refreshAfterDate.!scriptable.description: The property indicates when the widget can be refreshed again. The widget will not be refreshed before the date have been reached. It is not guaranteed that the widget will refresh at exactly the specified date. The refresh rate of a widget is partly up to iOS/iPadOS. For example, a widget may not refresh if the device is low on battery or the user is rarely looking at the widget. When the property is null the default refresh interval is used. Defaults to null.

send.!scriptable.description: Presents a screen from which the mail can be sent. The mail will not be sent until you have confirmed it from the presented screen.

addImageAttachment.!doc: Adds an image attachment to the mail.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: image
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Image to add to the mail.

addFileAttachment.!doc: Adds a file attachment to the mail.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to add to the mail.

addDataAttachment.!type: fn(data: Data, mimeType: string, filename: string)
addDataAttachment.!doc: Adds a data attachment to the mail.
addDataAttachment.!scriptable.description: When adding a data attachment to the mail, you are responsible for providing a valid MIME type and filename. It is advised to use addImageAttachment and addFileAttachment whenever possible.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: data
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Data representation of file to add to the mail.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: mimeType
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: MIME type of file represented by the data.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filename
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Name of the file represented by the data.

toRecipients.!scriptable.description: Array of recipients to send the mail to. Elements in the array should be e-mail addresses. You will have a chance to modify this before the mail is sent.

ccRecipients.!doc: Recipients to set CC on the mail.
ccRecipients.!scriptable.description: Array of recipients to set as CC on the mail. Elements in the array should be e-mail addresses. You will have a chance to modify this before the mail is sent.

bccRecipients.!doc: Recipients to set BCC on the mail.
bccRecipients.!scriptable.description: Array of recipients to set as BCC on the mail. Elements in the array should be e-mail addresses. You will have a chance to modify this before the mail is sent.

subject.!scriptable.description: Subject of the mail to send. You will have a chance to modify this before the mail is sent.

body.!scriptable.description: Body of the mail to send. You will have a chance to modify this before the mail is sent.

isBodyHTML.!scriptable.description: Set to true if the body of the mail is HTML. Defaults to false.

preferredSendingEmailAddress.!doc: Preferred email address to use in the from field.
preferredSendingEmailAddress.!scriptable.description: Sets the preferred email addressed to use when sending the mail. If no account with the preferred email address is set up, the default email address is used.

send.!scriptable.description: Presents a screen from which the message can be sent. The message will not be sent until you have confirmed it from the presented screen.

addImageAttachment.!doc: Adds an image attachment to the message.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: image
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Image to add to the message.

addFileAttachment.!doc: Adds a file attachment to the message.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of file to add to the message.

addDataAttachment.!type: fn(data: Data, uti: string, filename: string)
addDataAttachment.!doc: Adds a data attachment to the message.
addDataAttachment.!scriptable.description: When adding a data attachment to the message, you are responsible for providing a valid Uniform Type Identifier and filename. It is advised to use addImageAttachment and addFileAttachment whenever possible.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: data
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Data representation of file to add to the message.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: uti
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: UTI of file represented by the data.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filename
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Name of the file represented by the data.

recipients.!scriptable.description: Array of recipients to send the message to. Elements in the array should be phone numbers. You will have a chance to modify this before the message is sent.

body.!scriptable.description: Body of the message to send. You will have a chance to modify this before the message is sent.

filename.!doc: Path to file containing the module.
filename.!scriptable.description: This is the absolute path to the file containing the module.

schedule.!scriptable.description: When a new notification is constructed, it must be scheduled, otherwise it will not be delivered. If an existing notification is modified, it must also be scheduled again for the changes to take effect.

remove.!scriptable.description: Removes all future triggers of the notification.

setTriggerDate.!doc: Sets the notification to be triggered on a date and time.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: date
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Date and time to trigger the notification on.

setDailyTrigger.!type: fn(hour: number, minute: number, repeats: bool)
setDailyTrigger.!doc: Sets the notification to be triggered daily.
setDailyTrigger.!scriptable.description: Sets the notification to be triggered on a specific time of the day. When the notification repeats, it will be sent at the same time on all future days. If the notification is not repating it will be sent on the next occurrence of the specified time.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: hour
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Hour of the day to trigger the notification.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: minute
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Minute of the day to trigger the notification.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: repeats
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: If true the notification will be sent daily on the specified time, otherwise it will only be sent once. Defaults to false.

setWeeklyTrigger.!type: fn(weekday: number, hour: number, minute: number, repeats: bool)
setWeeklyTrigger.!doc: Sets the notification to be triggered weekly.
setWeeklyTrigger.!scriptable.description: Sets the notification to be triggered on a specific day of the week and a specific time of that day. When the notification repeats, it will be sent at the same time on all future days. If the notification is not repating it will be sent on the next occurrence of the specified time.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: weekday
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Day of the week to trigger the notification.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: hour
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Hour of the day to trigger the notification.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: minute
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Minute of the day to trigger the notification.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: repeats
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: If true the notification will be sent daily on the specified time, otherwise it will only be sent once. Defaults to false.

addAction.!type: fn(title: string, url: string, destructive: bool)
addAction.!scriptable.description: Actions are shown as buttons in the notification. When screen space is unlimited, the system shows up to 10 actions. When the space is limited the system shows at most two actions.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: title
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Title of the action.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: url
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: URL to open when choosing the action.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: destructive
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Optional. If set to true, the button is displayed with special highlighting to indicate that it performs a destructive task. Defaults to false.

identifier.!scriptable.description: To reschedule a notification, use the identifier of an existing notification.

preferredContentHeight.!scriptable.description: By default Scriptable attempts to determine an appropriate height for your notification. If you want to override the default behavior, you can specify a preferred content height. The preferred content height is only used when running a script inside the notification, i.e. when scriptName is not null. iOS may limit the height of the notification in which case the preferred content height is not guaranteed to be respected.

badge.!doc: Number to display in the app icon's badge.
badge.!scriptable.description: When the number is zero, no badge is displayed. When the number is greater than zero, the number is displayed in the app icon's badge. Setting the value to null, will leave the badge unchanged. The default value is null.

threadIdentifier.!scriptable.description: Notifications are grouped by the identifier on the Home screen and in the Notification Center.

userInfo.!scriptable.description: Store any custom information for the notification. This can be accessed from the Notification.opened property when a script is run from a notification.

sound.!scriptable.description:
Set to null if you do not want any sound. Set to one of the following values if you want a sound.

- default
- accept
- alert
- complete
- event
- failure
- piano_error
- piano_success
- popup

By default the notification is delivered with no sound.

openURL.!doc: URL to open when notification is tapped.
openURL.!scriptable.description: The Scriptable application will open the URL when the notification is tapped. This can be a URL that uses Scriptables URL scheme, the URL scheme of another application or a website URL.

deliveryDate.!scriptable.description: If the notification has already been delivered, for example because it was fetched using Notification.allDelivered(), the deliveryDate will be populated. Otherwise it will be null. The property cannot be set. In order to specify a future delivery date for a notification, see the setTriggerDate function. For recurring notifications, see the setDailyTrigger and setWeeklyTrigger functions.

nextTriggerDate.!doc: Next trigger date of the notification.
nextTriggerDate.!scriptable.description: The next trigger date is the point in time where the next notification will be delivered. The property cannot be set. In order to specify a future delivery date for a notification, see the setTriggerDate function. For recurring notifications, see the setDailyTrigger and setWeeklyTrigger functions.

scriptName.!doc: Name of script to run in rich notification.
scriptName.!scriptable.description: When notification is force touched or long pressed, Scriptable can run a script inside the notification without opening the app. Set the scriptName to a name of an existing script to run it inside the notification.

move.!scriptable.description:
Moves to a point without drawing a line between the current
point and the new point.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: point
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Point to move to.

addLine.!doc: Adds a line to a point.
addLine.!scriptable.description:
Add a line from the current point, e.g. set using the move method,
and to the new point.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: point
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Point to add line to.

addRect.!scriptable.description: This is a convenience function for adding a rectangle to the path starting from the lower left corner and drawing the lines counter-clockwise until the rectangle is closed.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: rect
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Rectangle to add.

addEllipse.!scriptable.description: Adds an ellipse incapsulated by the provided rectangle to the path.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: rect
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Rectangle incapsulating the ellipse.

addRoundedRect.!type: fn(rect: Rect, cornerWidth: number, cornerHeight: number)
addRoundedRect.!scriptable.description: Adds a rounded rectangle to the path. The corner width specifies the horizontal size of the corner and the corner height specifies the the vertical size of the corner.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: rect
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Rectangle to add.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: cornerWidth
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Horizontal size of the rounded corner.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: cornerHeight
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Vertical size of the rounded corner.

addCurve.!type: fn(point: Point, control1: Point, control2: Point)
addCurve.!doc: Adds a cubic curve to a point.
addCurve.!scriptable.description: Adds a cubic Bezier curve to the path with the specified end point and control points.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: point
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: End point of the curve.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: control1
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: First control point of the curve.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: control2
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Second control point of the curve.

addQuadCurve.!doc: Adds a quadratic curve to a point.
addQuadCurve.!scriptable.description: Adds a quadratic Bezier curve to the specified end point with the specified control point.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: point
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: End point of the curve.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: control
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Control point of the curve.

addLines.!scriptable.description: Adds straight lines between an array of points. Calling this method is equivalent to calling the move function with the first point in the array of points and then calling addLine on the subsequent points in the array.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: points
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Points to add lines between.

addRects.!scriptable.description: Calling this is equivalent to repeatedly calling addRect.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: rects
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Rectangles to add.

closeSubpath.!scriptable.description: Adds a straight line from the current point to the start of the current subpath.

minX.!scriptable.description: The smallest x-coordinate in the rectangle.

minY.!scriptable.description: The smallest y-coordinate in the rectangle.

maxX.!scriptable.description: The greatest x-coordinate in the rectangle.

maxY.!scriptable.description: The greatest y-coordinate in the rectangle.

origin.!doc: Point that specifies the rectangles origin.
origin.!scriptable.description: The x- and y-coordinate that specifies the rectangles origin as a Point structure.

size.!scriptable.description: The width and height of the rectangle as a Size structure.

string.!doc: Creates a localized string communicating the amount of time between two dates.
string.!scriptable.description: Creates a localized textual representation of the amount of time between to dates. If the two dates are the same, the function will return "now". If the reference date is yesterday, the function will return "yesterday". Other examples include "in 10 seconds", "2 hours ago", "last week" and "next year".

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: date
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: The date to create a relative date and time for.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: referenceDate
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: The reference date that date is relative to.

useNamedDateTimeStyle.!scriptable.description:
When using the named style, the formatter tries to find a suitable textual representation over a numeric value for the relative time, e.g. "now" instead of "in 0 seconds" and "yesterday" instead of "1 day ago".

When no named representation is found the formatter will fallback to using the numeric style.

useNumericDateTimeStyle.!scriptable.description: When using the numeric style, the formatter will always prefer numeric representations over named representations. E.g. it will return "in 0 seconds" instead of "now" and "1 day ago" instead of "yesteday".

locale.!scriptable.description: The locale should be specified using a string identifier, e.g. "en", "it" or "da". When no locale is set, the formatter will use the current locale of the device.

addRecurrenceRule.!scriptable.description: Recurrence rules specify when the reminder should be repeated. See the documentation of RecurrenceRule for more information on creating rules.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: recurrenceRule
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Recurrence rule to add to the reminder.

save.!scriptable.description: Saves changes to a reminder, inserting it into the calendar if it is newly created.

priority.!scriptable.description: Specifies the prirority of the reminder with 0 representing an undefined priority, 1 the highest priority, and 9 the lowest priority.

dueDateIncludesTime.!doc: Whether the due date includes a time.
dueDateIncludesTime.!scriptable.description: When this is true, assignments to the dueDate property will include a time, when this is false, the time component of the date will be ignored. Defaults to true.

calendar.!doc: Calendar the reminder is stored in.

load.!scriptable.description: Call to send the configured request to the specified URL. The raw response is provided when the returned promise is fulfilled.

loadString.!doc: Sends request and parses response as a string.
loadString.!scriptable.description: Call to send the configured request to the specified URL. The response is parsed to a string and provided when the returned promise is fulfilled.

loadJSON.!doc: Sends request and parses response as JSON.
loadJSON.!scriptable.description: Call to send the configured request to the specified URL. The response is expected to be a valid JSON string and is parsed into an object.

loadImage.!doc: Sends request and parses response as an image.
loadImage.!scriptable.description: Call to send the configured request to the specified URL. The response is expected to be an image.

addParameterToMultipart.!doc: Adds a parameter to a multipart request.
addParameterToMultipart.!scriptable.description: Converts the request to a multipart request and adds a parameter with the specified name and value. Be aware that the body property on the request is ignored for multipart requests as parameters and files added to the request constitutes the body. Calling this function will make the request a multipart request. When the request is send, the content type will automatically be set to "multipart/form-data".

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: name
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Name of the parameter.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: value
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Value of the parameter.

addFileDataToMultipart.!type: fn(data: Data, mimeType: string, name: string, filename: string)
addFileDataToMultipart.!doc: Adds a file to a multipart request.
addFileDataToMultipart.!scriptable.description: Converts the request to a multipart request and adds the file to the request. Be aware that the body property on the request is ignored for multipart requests as parameters and files added to the request constitutes the body. Calling this function will make the request a multipart request. When the request is send, the content type will automatically be set to "multipart/form-data".

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: data
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: File data to add.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: mimeType
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: MIME type of the file to add.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: name
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Name of the parameter which holds the file.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filename
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Name of the file.

addFileToMultipart.!type: fn(filePath: string, name: string, filename: string)
addFileToMultipart.!doc: Adds a file to a multipart request.
addFileToMultipart.!scriptable.description: Converts the request to a multipart request and adds the file to the request. The function will automatically determine the MIME type of the file as well as the filename. Be aware that the body property on the request is ignored for multipart requests as parameters and files added to the request constitutes the body. Calling this function will make the request a multipart request. When the request is send, the content type will automatically be set to "multipart/form-data".

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filePath
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Path of the file to add.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: name
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Name of the parameter which holds the file.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filename
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Optional name of the uploaded file.

addImageToMultipart.!type: fn(image: Image, name: string, filename: string)
addImageToMultipart.!doc: Adds an image to a multipart request.
addImageToMultipart.!scriptable.description: Converts the request to a multipart request and adds the image to the request. The function will automatically determine the MIME type of the file Be aware that the body property on the request is ignored for multipart requests as parameters and files added to the request constitutes the body. Calling this function will make the request a multipart request. When the request is send, the content type will automatically be set to "multipart/form-data".

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: image
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Image to add.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: name
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Name of the parameter which holds the file.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: filename
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Optional name of the uploaded file.

method.!doc: HTTP method used for the request.
method.!scriptable.description: Specifies the HTTP method to use when sending the request. The default is to send the request using the GET HTTP method.

headers.!doc: HTTP headers to send with the request.
headers.!scriptable.description: Key value pairs where the key is the name of an HTTP header and the value will be sent as the value for the HTTP header.

body.!doc: Body to send with the request.
body.!scriptable.description: The body will be send along the request. While this property can be any value, currently only strings and Data is supported. Be aware that this property is ignored if you convert the request to a multipart request using addParameterToMultipart, addFileToMultipart or addFileDataToMultipart.

timeoutInterval.!scriptable.description:
If a request remains idle for longer than the timeout interval, the request is considered timed out.

The timeout interval is measured in seconds and defaults to 60 seconds.

onRedirect.!scriptable.description: The function determines how redirects should be handled. By default redirects are allowed. When invoked the function is supplied with the request that we're about to redirect to. The function can return the request to continue redirecting or it can return another request to redirect to.
