Returning null will stop the redirect. Note that onRedirect will only be invoked on the initial request. Consecutive redirects should be handled on the initial request.

allowInsecureRequest.!doc: Allow the request even if it is deemed insecure.
allowInsecureRequest.!scriptable.description:
By default Scriptable will attempt to reject requests that are deemed insecure.

As an example, Scriptable will reject communicating with a server that has an invalid certificate. Such servers might be malicious and may put confidentional information at risk. By enabling this setting, those requests will be allowed.

Enable this setting at your own risk.

applyFont.!doc: Configures the symbol with the specified font information.

applyUltraLightWeight.!doc: Configures the symbol to use an ultra light weight.

applyThinWeight.!doc: Configures the symbol to use an thin weight.

applyLightWeight.!doc: Configures the symbol to use an light weight.

applyRegularWeight.!doc: Configures the symbol to use an regular weight.

applyMediumWeight.!doc: Configures the symbol to use an medium weight.

applySemiboldWeight.!doc: Configures the symbol to use an semibold weight.

applyBoldWeight.!doc: Configures the symbol to use an bold weight.

applyHeavyWeight.!doc: Configures the symbol to use an heavy weight.

applyBlackWeight.!doc: Configures the symbol to use an black weight.

image.!doc: Convert the symbol to an image.

setDefaultKeyboard.!doc: Use the default keyboard for entering text.

setNumberPadKeyboard.!doc: Use a keyboard that prominently features the numbers 0 through 9.

setDecimalPadKeyboard.!doc: Use a numeric keyboard with a decimal point for entering text.

setNumbersAndPunctuationKeyboard.!doc: Use a numeric keyboard with punctuation for entering text.

setPhonePadKeyboard.!doc: Use a keyboard that prominently feaetures the numbers 0 through 9 and the * and # characters.

setWebSearchKeyboard.!doc: Use a keyboard that prominently features the space and period characters.

setEmailAddressKeyboard.!doc: Use a keyboard that prominently features the @, period and space characters.

setURLKeyboard.!doc: Use a keyboard that prominently faetures the period and slash characters and the ".com" string.

setTwitterKeyboard.!doc: Use a keyboard that prominently features the @ and # characters.

leftAlignText.!scriptable.description: This is the default text alignment.

placeholder.!doc: Placeholder shown in the text field while it is empty.

isSecure.!doc: Hides the text that is entered when set to true.

schedule.!scriptable.description: Schedules the timer using its configuration. The supplied function is called when the timer fires. To stop the timer from firing, call the invalidate() function.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: callback
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: The callback to be called when the timer fires.

invalidate.!scriptable.description: Stops the timer from firing ever again. Non-repeating timers are automatically invalidated after they have fired once. Repeating timers must be manually invalidated.

timeInterval.!doc: The frequency at which the timer fires, in milliseconds.
timeInterval.!scriptable.description: Be aware that the time interval is specified in setting. Defaults to 0, causing the timer to fire instantly.

repeats.!scriptable.description: A repeating timer will keep firing until it is invalidated. In contrast to non-repeating timers, repeating timers are not automatically invalidated. Defaults to false.

addRow.!scriptable.description: Adds a row to the table. Rows are shown vertically in the table view, i.e. from the top and down. Rows are rendered in the order they are added.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: row
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Row to add.

removeRow.!scriptable.description: Removes a row from the table.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: row
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Row to remove.

removeAllRows.!scriptable.description: Removes all rows from the table. If the table is presented, you must call the reload function in order for the changes to be reflected visually.

reload.!scriptable.description: If you add or remove rows while a table view is presented, you must reload the table in order for the changes to take effect.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: fullscreen
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Optional. Set to true to present the web view in fullscreen. This only has an effect when used within the app. Defaults to false.

showSeparators.!scriptable.description: Whether to show separators between rows. Defaults to false.

leftAligned.!scriptable.description: Specifies that content in the cell should be left aligned.

centerAligned.!scriptable.description: Specifies that content in the cell should be center aligned.

rightAligned.!scriptable.description: Specifies that content in the cell should be right aligned.

widthWeight.!scriptable.description:
A width weight specifies the relative width of the cell. When computing the absolute width of the cell, all width weights are taken into account. Consider the following example.

Cell A has a width weight of 50.
Cell B has a width weight of 100.
Cell C has a width wegiht of 150.

Assume that the row has an absolute width of 100. The width will be distributed among cells A, B and C. B will be double as wide as A but C will be fifty percent wider than B and three times as wide as A.

onTap.!doc: !prop_fn Called when the button is tapped.
onTap.!scriptable.description: Buttons cannot be tapped when the table is presented in Siri.

dismissOnTap.!doc: Whether to dismiss the table when the button is tapped.

titleColor.!scriptable.description: This only has an effect on cells with a title. By default the color is null, in which case an appropriate color is automatically chosen based on the theme of the app and the context the script is running in.

subtitleColor.!scriptable.description: This only has an effect on cells with a subtitle. By default the color is null, in which case an appropriate color is automatically chosen based on the theme of the app and the context the script is running in.

addCell.!scriptable.description: Adds a cell to the row. Note that cells are shown in the order they are added to the row.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: cell
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Cell to add to the row.

addText.!scriptable.description: Constructs a new cell containing the specified string and adds it to the row.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: title
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Optional title to show in the cell.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: subtitle
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Optional subtitle shown below the title in the cell.

addImage.!scriptable.description: Constructs a new cell containing the specified image and adds it to the row.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: image
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Image to show in the cell.

addImageAtURL.!scriptable.description: Constructs a new cell that loads the image at the specified url and adds the cell to the row.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: url
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: URL to image.

addButton.!scriptable.description: Constructs a new cell that contains a button. Set the onTap property to specify an action to performed when the button is tapped.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: title
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Title of the button.

cellSpacing.!scriptable.description: Specifies the horizontal spacing between cells in the row.

height.!scriptable.description: The height of the row defaults to 44.

isHeader.!doc: Whether the cell is a header.
isHeader.!scriptable.description: Headers are highlighted cells that helps users understand context. Defaults to false.

dismissOnSelect.!doc: Whether to dismiss the table when the row is selected.
dismissOnSelect.!scriptable.description: This property will only have an effect if the row is selectable, i.e. onSelect has a value. Otherwise it is ignored. Defaults to true.

onSelect.!doc: !prop_fn Called when the row is selected.
onSelect.!scriptable.description:
Called when the row is selected when the table is presented. If this has no value, the row cannot be selected. Defaults to null.

Rows cannot be tapped when the tables is presented in Siri.

loadURL.!scriptable.description: Loads the URL in the web view. The returned promise will complete once the web view has finished loading.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: url
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: URL to load into the web view.

loadRequest.!scriptable.description: When loading a request into the web view, the HTTP method, body and headers of the request will be respected. The onRedirect function on the request will not be invoked.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: request
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Request to load into the web view.

loadHTML.!scriptable.description: Loads the HTML into  the web view. The returned promise will complete once the web view has finished loading.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: html
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: HTML to load into the web view.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: baseURL
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Optional. Base URL used to resolve relative URLs in the HTML.

loadFile.!doc: Loads file in the web view.
loadFile.!scriptable.description:
Files can be of various types, including HTML files and images.

The supplied HTML file can reference files and nested directories in the same directory as the HTML file resides.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: fileURL
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: URL of the file to load and render.

evaluateJavaScript.!doc: Evaluates JavaScript in the web view.
evaluateJavaScript.!scriptable.description: Evaluates JavaScript in the current context of the web view. The returned promise carries the result of evaluating the JavaScript. When passing false to the useCallback parameter, which is the default value, evaluation will terminate after evaluating the last line of the JavaScript. The value on the last line of the script will be carried by the promise returned by evaluateJavaScript. When passing true to the useCallback parameter, evaluation will only complete after the globally available completion function is called. Any value passed to the function, will be carried by the promise returned by evaluateJavaScript. The log is available from the evaluated JavaScript, i.e. messages passed to the globally available log and logError functions will be shown in the log.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: javaScript
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: JavaScript to evaluate in the web view.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: useCallback
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Optional. If true the web view waits for the globally available completion function of the web view to be called before terminating. Defaults to false.

getHTML.!doc: Reads and returns HTML from the loaded website.

present.!scriptable.description: The web view is presented with the content that has been loaded into it.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: fullscreen
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Set to true to present the web view in fullscreen. Defaults to false.

waitForLoad.!doc: Waits for the web view to load.
waitForLoad.!scriptable.description: The returned promise will be fulfilled when the web view finishes loading. If the load fails, the promise will be fulfilled with an error. Use this with caution. If the web view is not loading a new page or is not about to load a new page, the returned promise will never be fulfilled. This limitation exists because Scriptable cannot determine if a web view is about to load a page in cases where evaluating JavaScript in the web view causes a new page to load. Generally this should only be used when loading causing a new page to load from evaluateJavaScript. In other cases, e.g. when loading a URL using loadURL, the returned promise will be fulfilled when the page have been loaded.

shouldAllowRequest.!doc: !prop_fn Function called upon load of a request.
shouldAllowRequest.!scriptable.description:
When the web view performs a request to load a resource, the function can determine whether or not to allow the request. Disallowing request can speed up the time it takes to load the website.

By default all requests are allowed.

leftAlignText.!scriptable.description:
Specifies that text should be left aligned. This is the default.

This does not affect texts placed in stacks. Use spacers instead when aligning text in stacks. To align the text to left right in a horizontal stack, you should place a spacer after the text.

centerAlignText.!scriptable.description:
Specifies that text should be center aligned.

This does not affect texts placed in stacks. Use spacers instead when aligning text in stacks. To align the text in the center of a horizontal stack, you should place a spacer both before and after the text.

rightAlignText.!scriptable.description:
Specifies that text should be right aligned.

This does not affect texts placed in stacks. Use spacers instead when aligning text in stacks. To align the text to the right in a horizontal stack, you should place a spacer before the text.

applyTimeStyle.!doc: Display time component of the date.

applyDateStyle.!scriptable.description:
Example output:
    June 3, 2019

This is the default.

applyRelativeStyle.!doc: Display date as relative to now.
applyRelativeStyle.!scriptable.description:
Example output:
    2 hours, 23 minutes
    1 year, 1 month

applyOffsetStyle.!doc: Display date as offset from now.

applyTimerStyle.!doc: Display date as timer counting from now.

date.!doc: Date to show in a widget.

font.!doc: Font and text size of the text.

textOpacity.!scriptable.description: Opacity of the text. This must be a value between 0 and 1. Defaults to 1.

lineLimit.!scriptable.description: Maximum number of lines to display. The limit is disabled when the value is 0 or less. Defaults to 0.

minimumScaleFactor.!doc: Minimum amount the text scales down to.
minimumScaleFactor.!scriptable.description: Sets the minimum amount that text scales down to fit in the available space. For example, a text with a minimum scale factor of 0.5 allows the widget to draw the text in a font size half the size of the actual font. The scale factor should be a fraction between 0 and 1, both inclusive. Defaults to 1.

shadowColor.!scriptable.description: Sets the color of the shadow cast by the text. The shadowRadius property must have a value greater than zero for this property to have an effect. Defaults to black.

shadowRadius.!scriptable.description: Sets the size of the shadow cast by the text. Defaults to 0.

shadowOffset.!scriptable.description: Sets the offset of the shadow cast by the text. The shadowRadius property must have a value greater than zero for this property to have an effect. Defaults to (0, 0).

url.!scriptable.description: The URL will be opened when the text is tapped. This is only supported in medium and large widgets. Small widgets can only have a single tap target, which is specified by the url on the widget.

leftAlignImage.!scriptable.description: Specifies that image should be left aligned. This is the default.

centerAlignImage.!scriptable.description: Specifies that image should be center aligned.

rightAlignImage.!scriptable.description: Specifies that image should be right aligned.

applyFittingContentMode.!scriptable.description: The image will fit the available space. This content mode is the default.

applyFillingContentMode.!scriptable.description: The image will fill the available space.

resizable.!scriptable.description: When set to true, the image can be resized. Defaults to true.

imageSize.!doc: Size of the image in the widget.
imageSize.!scriptable.description: Size of the image. When set to null, the image will be shwon at its full size. Defaults to null.

imageOpacity.!scriptable.description: Opacity of the image. This must be a value between 0 and 1. Defaults to 1.

cornerRadius.!scriptable.description: Radius of the rounded corners. This property is ignored when containerRelativeShape is set to true. Defaults to 0.

borderWidth.!scriptable.description: Width of the border around the image. Defaults to 0.

borderColor.!scriptable.description: Color of the border around the image. Defaults to black.

containerRelativeShape.!doc: Shape the image relative to its container.
containerRelativeShape.!scriptable.description: When true the corners of the image will be rounded relative to the containing widget. The value of cornerRadius is ignored when this is true. Defaults to false.

tintColor.!scriptable.description: Changes the color of the image. Set to null to show the original image. Defaults to null.

url.!scriptable.description: The URL will be opened when the text is tapped. This is only supported in medium and large widgets. Small widgets can only have a single tap target, which is specified by the url on the widget.

addText.!scriptable.description: Adds a text element to the stack. Use the properties on the returned element to style the text.

addDate.!scriptable.description: Adds a date element to the widget. Use the properties on the returned element to style the date.

addImage.!scriptable.description: Adds an image element to the stack. Use the properties on the returned element to style the image.

addSpacer.!scriptable.description: Adds a spacer to the stack. This can be used to offset the content horizontally in the stack.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: length
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Length of the spacer. Pass null to create a spacer with a flexible length.

addStack.!scriptable.description: Adds a stack to the widget. Stacks layout elements horizontally by default.

setPadding.!type: fn(top: number, leading: number, bottom: number, trailing: number)
setPadding.!scriptable.description: Sets the padding on each side of the stack.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: top
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Padding on the top edge.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: leading
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Padding on the leading edge.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: bottom
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Padding on the bottom edge.

!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.name: trailing
!scriptable.parameters.!scriptable.description: Padding on the trailing edge.

useDefaultPadding.!scriptable.description: Configure the stack to use the default padding. Any padding previously defined with setPadding() will be discarded.

topAlignContent.!scriptable.description: Specifies that content should be top aligned. This is the default.

centerAlignContent.!scriptable.description: Specifies that content should be center aligned.

bottomAlignContent.!scriptable.description: Specifies that content should be bottom aligned.

layoutHorizontally.!scriptable.description: Specifies that the stack should layout elements horizontally. This is the default.

layoutVertically.!scriptable.description: Specifies that the stack should layout elements vertically.

spacing.!scriptable.description: Specifies the spacing between elements in the stack. You can also use the addSpacer() function on the widget to add spacing between elements. Defaults to 0.

size.!scriptable.description: Specifies the size of the stack when shown in a widget. When a dimension is set to zero or less, the widget will automatically decide a length for that dimension. Both dimensions default to 0.

cornerRadius.!scriptable.description: Radius of the rounded corners. Defaults to 0.

borderWidth.!scriptable.description: Width of the border around the stack. Defaults to 0.

borderColor.!scriptable.description: Color of the border around the stack. Defaults to black.

url.!scriptable.description: The URL will be opened when the text is tapped. This is only supported in medium and large widgets. Small widgets can only have a single tap target, which is specified by the url on the widget.

leftAlignText.!scriptable.description:
Specifies that text should be left aligned. This is the default.

This does not affect texts placed in stacks. Use spacers instead when aligning text in stacks. To align the text to left right in a horizontal stack, you should place a spacer after the text.

centerAlignText.!scriptable.description:
Specifies that text should be center aligned.

This does not affect texts placed in stacks. Use spacers instead when aligning text in stacks. To align the text in the center of a horizontal stack, you should place a spacer both before and after the text.

rightAlignText.!scriptable.description:
Specifies that text should be right aligned.

This does not affect texts placed in stacks. Use spacers instead when aligning text in stacks. To align the text to the right in a horizontal stack, you should place a spacer before the text.

text.!doc: Text to show in a widget.

font.!doc: Font and text size of the text.

lineLimit.!scriptable.description: Maximum number of lines to display. The limit is disabled when the value is 0 or less. Defaults to 0.

For example, a text with a minimum scale factor of 0.5 allows the widget to draw the text in a font size half the size of the actual font. The scale factor should be a fraction between 0 and 1, both inclusive. Defaults to 1.

shadowColor.!scriptable.description: Sets the color of the shadow cast by the text. The shadowRadius property must have a value greater than zero for this property to have an effect. Defaults to black.

shadowRadius.!scriptable.description: Sets the size of the shadow cast by the text. Defaults to 0.

shadowOffset.!scriptable.description: Sets the offset of the shadow cast by the text. The shadowRadius property must have a value greater than zero for this property to have an effect. Defaults to (0, 0).

parse.!scriptable.description: Before calling this function you should ensure that the parser is correctly configured, i.e. the necessary callback functions should be set.

didStartDocument.!doc: !prop_fn Function called when the parser begins parsing a document.

didEndDocument.!doc: !prop_fn Function called when the parser ends parsing a document.
didEndDocument.!scriptable.description: When the parser calls the function, it has successfully completed parsing the document.

didStartElement.!doc: !prop_fn Function called when starting to parse an element.
didStartElement.!scriptable.description:
Called by the parser when it encounters a start tag for an element. The function takes the element name as a parameter as well as a key value pair containing all the attributes associated with the element.

Use this function to update your state and prepare for receiving the characters of the element. After this function is called, the parser will call the foundCharacters callback function with all or parts of the characters of the element.

didEndElement.!doc: !prop_fn Function called when ended parsing an element.
didEndElement.!scriptable.description: Called by the parser when it encounters an end tag for an element. The function takes the element name as a parameter.

foundCharacters.!doc: !prop_fn Function called when the parser finds characters of an element.
foundCharacters.!scriptable.description: The parser calls this function with a string whenever it finds characters for the current element. This function may be called several times for a single element.

parseErrorOccurred.!doc: !prop_fn Function called when the parser encounters an error.
parseErrorOccurred.!scriptable.description: The parser will call this function when it encounters a fatal error preventing it from continuing to parse. When the function is called the parsing is stopped.
